name: Al-Fatihah
overview: The opening prayer of the Quran, recited in every unit of prayer. A concise supplication for divine guidance.

name: Al-Baqarah
overview: The longest surah, covering faith, law, stories of earlier prophets, and detailed guidance for the Muslim community.

name: Ali 'Imran
overview: Discusses the family of Imran, the birth of Isa (Jesus), the Battle of Uhud, and steadfastness in faith.

name: An-Nisa
overview: Addresses women's rights, inheritance laws, marriage, orphans' welfare, and justice within the community.

name: Al-Ma'idah
overview: Covers dietary laws, fulfilling covenants, the stories of earlier nations, and the completion of the religion.

name: Al-An'am
overview: A powerful declaration of tawhid (monotheism), refuting idolatry and presenting signs of Allah in creation.

name: Al-A'raf
overview: Recounts the stories of Adam, Nuh, Hud, Salih, Lut, Shu'ayb, and Musa, and the consequences of rejecting prophets.

name: Al-Anfal
overview: Revealed after the Battle of Badr, addressing the distribution of war gains and the ethics of warfare.

name: At-Tawbah
overview: Addresses relations with polytheists, the hypocrites, and obligations of the Muslim community. The only surah without Bismillah.

name: Yunus
overview: Named after Prophet Yunus, discussing the Quran's divine origin, earlier prophets, and Allah's mercy.

name: Hud
overview: Tells the stories of Nuh, Hud, Salih, Ibrahim, Lut, Shu'ayb, and Musa, emphasizing patience and perseverance.

name: Yusuf
overview: The detailed and continuous narrative of Prophet Yusuf — a story of patience, temptation, and divine wisdom.

name: Ar-Ra'd
overview: Reflects on thunder as a sign of Allah's power, the truth of revelation, and the fate of believers and deniers.

name: Ibrahim
overview: Prophet Ibrahim's prayers, gratitude for blessings, and warnings against ingratitude and disbelief.

name: Al-Hijr
overview: References the rocky tract of Thamud, the creation of Adam, and Iblis's refusal to prostrate.

name: An-Nahl
overview: Enumerates Allah's countless blessings — the bee, livestock, rain, and natural provisions — calling for gratitude.

name: Al-Isra
overview: Opens with the Night Journey (Isra) of the Prophet, followed by moral commandments and the excellence of the Quran.

name: Al-Kahf
overview: Contains four famous narratives: the People of the Cave, the two garden owners, Musa and Khidr, and Dhul-Qarnayn.

name: Maryam
overview: Recounts the births of Yahya and Isa, and stories of Ibrahim, Musa, Isma'il, and Idris.

name: Taha
overview: Centers on the story of Prophet Musa — from his calling at the burning bush to the confrontation with Pharaoh.

name: Al-Anbya
overview: Presents numerous prophets and their struggles, emphasizing that all messengers called to the same message of tawhid.

name: Al-Hajj
overview: Discusses the Hajj pilgrimage, resurrection, divine judgment, and permission for self-defense.

name: Al-Mu'minun
overview: Describes the qualities of successful believers and recounts stories of earlier prophets and their peoples.

name: An-Nur
overview: Establishes laws of modesty, the punishment for slander, the Light Verse (Ayat an-Nur), and social etiquette.

name: Al-Furqan
overview: Defends the Quran against objections, describes the qualities of the servants of the Most Merciful.

name: Ash-Shu'ara
overview: Retells the stories of Musa, Ibrahim, Nuh, Hud, Salih, Lut, and Shu'ayb in a rhythmic, powerful style.

name: An-Naml
overview: Features the stories of Sulayman with the hoopoe bird and the Queen of Sheba, and Musa and Salih.

name: Al-Qasas
overview: The detailed story of Musa from birth through Pharaoh's defeat, and the story of Qarun's arrogance.

name: Al-'Ankabut
overview: Uses the metaphor of the spider's fragile web to illustrate false beliefs, emphasizing trials test true faith.

name: Ar-Rum
overview: Prophesies the Roman victory after defeat, and reflects on signs of Allah in creation and human history.

name: Luqman
overview: Contains the wise advice of Luqman to his son on monotheism, gratitude, patience, and good character.

name: As-Sajdah
overview: Affirms the truth of revelation, describes creation, and contrasts the fate of believers and disbelievers.

name: Al-Ahzab
overview: Addresses the Battle of the Trench, rules for the Prophet's household, and social reforms including hijab.

name: Saba
overview: Named after the kingdom of Sheba, discussing divine blessings, gratitude, and the story of Dawud and Sulayman.

name: Fatir
overview: Praises Allah as the Creator and Originator, contrasting light and darkness, and the seen and unseen.

name: Ya-Sin
overview: A surah of great virtue, often called the 'Heart of the Quran.' Addresses resurrection, the signs of Allah, and the certainty of the hereafter.

name: As-Saffat
overview: Describes angels standing in ranks, recounts stories of prophets, and affirms the Day of Judgment.

name: Sad
overview: Discusses Dawud's judgment, Sulayman's kingdom, Ayyub's patience, and the story of Adam and Iblis.

name: Az-Zumar
overview: Emphasizes sincerity in worship, the contrast between believers and disbelievers, and Allah's mercy.

name: Ghafir
overview: Features a believing man from Pharaoh's family who concealed his faith while defending Musa, and emphasizes Allah's forgiveness.

name: Fussilat
overview: Presents the Quran as a detailed revelation, describes creation, and warns against rejecting divine signs.

name: Ash-Shuraa
overview: Discusses consultation (shura) as a principle, divine revelation, and unity in the message of all prophets.

name: Az-Zukhruf
overview: Refutes the worship of false gods, recounts Ibrahim's arguments against idolatry, and mentions Isa.

name: Ad-Dukhan
overview: Warns of a day when the sky will bring visible smoke, recounts Pharaoh's destruction, and the hereafter.

name: Al-Jathiyah
overview: Presents signs of Allah in creation and warns those who mock divine revelation of accountability.

name: Al-Ahqaf
overview: Named after the sand dunes where the people of 'Ad lived, stressing the Quran's truth and patience.

name: Muhammad
overview: Named after the Prophet Muhammad, discussing fighting in Allah's cause and the nature of hypocrites.

name: Al-Fath
overview: Revealed after the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, declaring it a clear victory and promising future conquests.

name: Al-Hujurat
overview: Teaches manners and ethics — avoiding suspicion, backbiting, and mockery — and affirms human equality.

name: Qaf
overview: Opens with an oath by the Quran, discusses creation, death, resurrection, and Allah's closeness to mankind.

name: Adh-Dhariyat
overview: Swears by the scattering winds, recounts stories of Ibrahim's guests, Musa, and destroyed nations.

name: At-Tur
overview: Swears by Mount Sinai, describes the bliss of Paradise, and refutes the disbelievers' accusations.

name: An-Najm
overview: Describes the Prophet's ascension (Mi'raj), refutes idol worship, and affirms divine authority.

name: Al-Qamar
overview: Opens with the splitting of the moon, retells stories of destroyed nations, and repeats the refrain of easy remembrance.

name: Ar-Rahman
overview: Enumerates Allah's countless blessings with the repeated refrain: 'Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?'

name: Al-Waqi'ah
overview: Describes the three groups on the Day of Judgment: the foremost, the companions of the right, and the left.

name: Al-Hadid
overview: Reflects on Allah's sovereignty over the heavens and earth, the nature of worldly life, and spending in charity.

name: Al-Mujadila
overview: Opens with a woman's complaint to the Prophet about her husband, addressing unjust divorce customs.

name: Al-Hashr
overview: Describes the exile of the Banu Nadir, the distribution of gains, and concludes with Allah's beautiful names.

name: Al-Mumtahanah
meaning: She that is to be examined
overview: Guides believers on relations with non-hostile and hostile disbelievers, and the testing of emigrant women.

name: As-Saf
overview: Calls believers to stand firm in ranks for Allah's cause and mentions Isa's prophecy of a messenger after him.

name: Al-Jumu'ah
overview: Discusses Friday congregational prayer and warns against being distracted by commerce during worship.

name: Al-Munafiqun
overview: Exposes the deception of the hypocrites in Madinah and their undermining of the Muslim community.

name: At-Taghabun
overview: Reminds of Allah's knowledge of all things, the test of wealth and children, and mutual loss and gain.

name: At-Talaq
overview: Details the rules and waiting periods of divorce, emphasizing fairness and consciousness of Allah.

name: At-Tahrim
overview: Addresses an incident in the Prophet's household and uses the examples of righteous and unrighteous women.

name: Al-Mulk
overview: Reflects on Allah's dominion over creation, the perfection of the heavens, and accountability after death.

name: Al-Qalam
overview: One of the earliest revelations. Defends the Prophet's character and tells the parable of the garden owners.

name: Al-Haqqah
overview: Vividly describes the Day of Judgment, the destruction of 'Ad and Thamud, and the weighing of deeds.

name: Al-Ma'arij
overview: Describes the Day when angels ascend, the nature of human impatience, and the qualities of the righteous.

name: Nuh
overview: Prophet Nuh's long call to his people, their persistent rejection, and his prayer against them.

name: Al-Jinn
overview: Describes a group of jinn who heard the Quran, believed in it, and returned to warn their people.

name: Al-Muzzammil
overview: Commands the Prophet to stand in night prayer, emphasizing devotion and patience in the early mission.

name: Al-Muddaththir
overview: One of the earliest revelations, commanding the Prophet to rise and warn, and describing the hereafter.

name: Al-Qiyamah
overview: Affirms the resurrection, describes the soul's departure at death, and Allah's power to recreate.

name: Al-Insan
overview: Describes the creation of man, the rewards of the righteous in Paradise, and Allah's will over all things.

name: Al-Mursalat
overview: Describes the winds sent forth, the Day of Judgment, and repeatedly asks: 'Woe that Day to the deniers.'

name: An-Naba
overview: Presents evidence for resurrection through signs in creation — mountains, sleep, rain, and the heavens.

name: An-Nazi'at
overview: Describes the angels who extract souls, the Day of Judgment, and retells Musa's encounter with Pharaoh.

name: 'Abasa
overview: Addresses the Prophet for turning away from a blind man, emphasizing that guidance is for all who seek it.

name: At-Takwir
overview: Depicts the cosmic upheaval of the Last Day — the sun folding up, stars falling, and souls being paired.

name: Al-Infitar
overview: Describes the breaking apart of the sky and seas on the Day of Judgment, and the recording of all deeds.

name: Al-Mutaffifin
overview: Warns those who cheat in weights and measures, and contrasts the record books of the wicked and righteous.

name: Al-Inshiqaq
overview: Describes the sky splitting open, the earth flattening, and people receiving their record in their right hand or behind their back.

name: Al-Buruj
overview: References the People of the Trench who were persecuted for their faith, and affirms Allah's encompassing power.

name: At-Tariq
overview: Swears by the night star, reflects on human creation from fluid, and affirms the Quran's decisive word.

name: Al-A'la
overview: Glorifies Allah the Most High, discusses the creation of all things, and the superiority of the hereafter.

name: Al-Ghashiyah
overview: Contrasts the faces of the wretched and the blessed on the Day of Judgment, and calls to reflect on creation.

name: Al-Fajr
overview: Swears by the dawn, recounts the destruction of 'Ad, Thamud, and Pharaoh, and warns against love of wealth.

name: Al-Balad
overview: Describes the struggles of life in this city (Makkah) and the steep path of righteousness — freeing slaves and feeding the poor.

name: Ash-Shams
overview: Swears by the sun and the soul, recounts the destruction of Thamud, and affirms that success lies in purifying the soul.

name: Al-Layl
overview: Contrasts those who give and fear Allah with those who are miserly, and affirms that Allah guides to what is easy.

name: Ad-Duhaa
overview: A comforting revelation to the Prophet during a period when revelation paused, assuring him of Allah's care.

name: Ash-Sharh
overview: Reassures the Prophet that Allah has expanded his chest, eased his burden, and that relief comes with hardship.

name: At-Tin
overview: Swears by the fig and olive, affirming that man was created in the best form but can fall to the lowest.

name: Al-'Alaq
overview: Contains the first verses revealed to the Prophet — the command to read (Iqra) — and warns against arrogance.

name: Al-Qadr
overview: Declares the Night of Decree (Laylatul Qadr) better than a thousand months, when angels descend with peace.

name: Al-Bayyinah
overview: Distinguishes between the People of the Book who believed and those who rejected the clear evidence.

name: Az-Zalzalah
overview: Describes the violent earthquake of the Last Day when the earth will reveal all that was done upon it.

name: Al-'Adiyat
overview: Swears by charging warhorses and describes man's ingratitude and love of wealth.

name: Al-Qari'ah
overview: Describes the striking calamity of the Day of Judgment when people will be like scattered moths.

name: At-Takathur
overview: Warns that competition for worldly increase distracts people until they visit their graves.

name: Al-'Asr
overview: A concise oath by time that mankind is in loss except those who believe, do good, and counsel truth and patience.

name: Al-Humazah
overview: Warns those who slander, backbite, and hoard wealth, describing the crushing fire that awaits them.

name: Al-Fil
overview: Recounts how Allah destroyed the army of Abraha and his elephants who tried to demolish the Ka'bah.

name: Quraysh
overview: Reminds the Quraysh of Allah's blessings — their trade caravans and security — and calls them to worship.

name: Al-Ma'un
overview: Rebukes those who deny the Day of Judgment, mistreat orphans, neglect the needy, and pray without sincerity.

name: Al-Kawthar
overview: Grants the Prophet the river of Al-Kawthar in Paradise and tells him to pray and sacrifice for Allah alone.

name: Al-Kafirun
overview: A clear declaration of separation between the Prophet's worship and that of the disbelievers.

name: An-Nasr
overview: The last surah revealed, acknowledging the conquest of Makkah and the spread of Islam, and commanding praise and seeking forgiveness.

name: Al-Masad
overview: Condemns Abu Lahab and his wife for their fierce opposition to the Prophet and the message of Islam.

name: Al-Ikhlas
overview: A concise declaration of pure monotheism — Allah is One, eternal, unbegotten, and without equal.

name: Al-Falaq
overview: A prayer seeking refuge in Allah from external harms — darkness, magic, and the envy of others.

name: An-Nas
overview: A prayer seeking refuge in Allah from the whispering of Shaytan — whether from jinn or mankind — into the hearts of people.
